content:
They're found a 6-ary star system, consisting of 3 binary stars:

  • Six stars, six eclipses: ‘the fact that it exists blows my mind’

At least one other 6-ary star system is known (called Castor). I guess no 7-ary star systems have been seen. There's probably some very hard theorem waiting to be proved, saying how less likely 7-star systems are than 6-star systems.
Is there a lot known about the 6-body problem?
I don't think so! But I don't know for sure. This particular star system is like a 3-body problem built from 2-body problems, and I bet that "hierarchical" systems are more likely to be stable.
